items.title: Inside the new No Surprises Act arbitration rules - Modern Healthcare News
items.reason: This article directly addresses the Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) arbitration process and its rules, which are core components of No Surprises Act implementation that revenue-cycle teams must navigate.
items.summary: Modern Healthcare News published an article examining the new arbitration rules under the No Surprises Act's Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) process. The IDR process is the federal mechanism through which disputes over out-of-network surprise bills are resolved when patients and insurers cannot agree on payment amounts. These rules directly impact how healthcare organizations and revenue-cycle teams handle balance-billing disputes and determine final payment obligations for surprise medical bills. Understanding the updated arbitration framework is critical for revenue-cycle operations managing claim denials and payment determinations under the Act.
